ingredients.prep: 14-15 oz (optional for more Creole tomato-forward style)
or a mix of seafood + chicken stock

steps.text: Prep: Dice onion, bell pepper, and celery. Mince garlic. Slice okra. Pat shrimp dry. If using oysters, keep them chilled with their liquor. Have stock warmed and ready.

Make a light-to-medium roux: In a heavy pot over medium heat, whisk oil and flour together. Cook, whisking constantly, until the roux turns peanut-butter to light caramel color (Creole gumbo often uses a lighter roux than Cajun).
Cook the trinity: Stir in onion, bell pepper, and celery (it will sizzle). Cook until softened and the roux thickens around the vegetables. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.
Add okra and optional tomatoes: Stir in okra and cook a few minutes. If using diced tomatoes, stir them in now.

Build the gumbo: Slowly add warm seafood stock while stirring to keep the gumbo smooth. Add bay leaves, thyme, oregano, paprika, cayenne, black pepper, salt, Worcestershire, and optional hot sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer and develop flavor: Simmer uncovered until the gumbo thickens slightly and flavors meld. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Skim excess oil if needed.
Add crab: Stir in crab and simmer briefly to heat through and infuse the broth.
Add shrimp near the end: Add shrimp and simmer gently just until pink and cooked through (avoid hard boiling).
Add oysters last (optional): Stir in oysters with their liquor and cook just until the edges curl and they are warmed through.
Finish: Remove bay leaves. Stir in lemon juice, most green onions, and most parsley. Taste and adjust salt and hot sauce.
Serve: Ladle gumbo over hot cooked rice. Garnish with remaining green onions and parsley. Serve extra hot sauce on the side.
